Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act and the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act
Identification.RunningHead: Post-Secondary Education Financial Assistance for Persons with Disabilities Act

Provision.Text: This enactment provides for financial assistance for Canadians with disabilities to improve their access to post-secondary education.
Whereas an estimated one in five Canadians aged 15 years and over has one or more disabilities that limit them in their daily activities;

Whereas the 2017 Canadian Survey on Disability stated that youth with disabilities are at a higher risk of not being in school or employed and that this risk increases with the severity of the disability;
And whereas Parliament recognizes the importance of ensuring that all Canadians with disabilities have access to post-secondary education;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Post-Secondary Education Financial Assistance for Persons with Disabilities Act.
Subsection 81(1) of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.1):

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: an amount paid under section 9.3 of the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act;

Section.Text: The Canada Student Financial Assistance Act is amended by adding the following after section 9.2:

Subsection.Text: Subject to any prescribed conditions, if an individual in respect of whom an amount is deductible under section 118.3 of the Income Tax Act is enrolled in a designated educational institution, the Minister shall pay that individualâs tuition fees to that designated educational institution.
The amount of any tuition fees paid under subsection (1) is deemed to be a grant to the individual on whose behalf it is paid.

Section.Text: Subsection 15(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (p):

Paragraph.Label: (p.1)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the conditions to be met before a payment under section 9.3 may be made;
